# High Cost Per Result: Diagnosing and Reversing CPR Inflation

CPR inflation is the gradual increase in advertising cost per desired action - click, lead, purchase - caused by audience saturation, creative fatigue, or bid competition.

## EVIDENCE BASE
- 72% of advertisers see CPR increase by more than 30% after 21 days without creative rotation - Meta Performance Insights 2024.
- Median CPR for B2B SaaS lead-gen on Meta is $18-$45; top quartile achieves below $12 - Metadata.io Benchmarks 2024.

## CORE TACTICS
### Creative Rotation Cadence
Rotate ad creative every 14-21 days or when frequency exceeds 3.0 per week.

### Audience Refresh
Exclude recent converters and re-seed lookalikes monthly.

## PERFORMANCE BENCHMARKS
| Metric | Poor | Median | Top Quartile | Source |
|--------|------|--------|--------------|--------|
| CPR (B2B lead-gen, Meta) | > $60 | $18-$45 | < $12 | Metadata.io 2024 |
| Creative frequency before fatigue | > 4.0 | 3.0 | 1.5-2.5 | Meta 2024 |

## COUNTER-INDICATORS
- **Nonprofit / grant-period campaigns:** CPR benchmarks do not apply during grant-restricted spend windows.

## VOCABULARY
- **Creative fatigue**: Declining CTR caused by repeated ad exposure to the same audience.
- **CPR**: Cost Per Result - the amount spent divided by the number of desired actions achieved.
